metadata.title:
WOULDN'T ALLOW TABLETS


WITH REUTERDAHL'S NAME

pages.raw_text:
An interesting fact in connection
works extensions, which will be
inquired into tomorrow night by
the city council, has COME to the
surface. After Arvid Reuterdahl,
fOrmc.' water commissioner, WHO
drew the plans. was removed from
office, the engineering supervision
of the job was turned over to J.
c. Ralston. The specifications,
among other things. called for the
placing of a tablet of bronze on
each job, containing the names of
the officials connected with the
building of the work. This is Gus
tomary on all public works, and
in the standpipes and reservoirs
there was no exception to the rule.
Acting on this requirement of the
contract, Huetter & Zirngibl in
charge of the work, had brass
plates made at an expense Of 200.
containing the names of the city
officials, included in the list being
that of A. Reuterdahl, designer Of
the plans and president of the
board of public works at the time.


When RalSton saw the tablets
and the name of Reuterdahl there
on he Immediately made objection.
in in that form, and the contractors
said that if new plates were made
the city must pay for them. AS a
result, the standpipes thus far
built have gone up without tablets.


One of the contractors said:
while those tablets cost us 5000
they were covered in the contract,
and the loss. if any. is that of the
city, not ours. OF course we are
not making any comments beyond
that it looked to us as if it were
small piece of business"
